url.description: Server URL for streamableHttp / sse transports.

env.description: Environment variables passed to stdio child processes.

authRef.description: Optional secret:// URI for bearer / header auth on HTTP transports.

tools.description: Optional tool allowlist. Null = expose all tools the server lists.

image.description: Container image reference. Mutually exclusive with Build.

context.description: Docker build context path. Relative paths resolve against the manifest file's directory at CLI time.

dockerfile.description: Dockerfile path relative to Context. Default Dockerfile.

push.description: Run docker push after a successful build. Default false.

build.description: Build-on-apply spec. The CLI builds the image and tags it before the manifest is sent. Mutually exclusive with Image only when neither is null.

port.description: Bridge HTTP port the container exposes. Default 7000 (chosen to dodge common dev ports — runtime 8080, plugin 8090, langfuse 3000, openwebui 5000).

healthPath.description: Bridge health endpoint path. Default /health; the runtime polls until it returns 2xx.

command.description: Optional override of the image's default CMD (i.e. the bridge entrypoint).

args.description: Optional args appended to Command (or image CMD).

env.description: Environment variables passed to the container. The bridge reads its stdio child command from MCP_STDIO_CMD by convention.

secrets.description: Secret references injected as env vars at container start. Key = env-var name; value = secret:// URI.

cpu.description: CPU limit in cores (e.g. 0.25, 1, 2). Null = supervisor default.

pidsLimit.description: Max process IDs the container may spawn. Null = supervisor default.

resources.description: Resource limits — memory / cpu / pids. Defaults apply when null.

startupTimeoutSeconds.description: Seconds to wait for the bridge HealthPath to return 2xx during initial start. Default 30.

imagePullPolicy.description: Docker image pull policy: Always | IfNotPresent | Never. Default IfNotPresent.

serviceUrl.description: URL of the K8s Service the runtime should probe (e.g. http://my-mcp.default.svc.cluster.local:7000).

deploymentName.description: K8s Deployment name (used for image patching).

kubernetes.description: Kubernetes-specific config (required when the runtime supervisor is the K8s one).

container.description: Container spec for containerStdio transport. Required when Transport is containerStdio; must be null for other transports.

virtual.description: True = virtual aggregator. False (default) = physical server.

ref.description: The Id of the upstream server.

sources.description: Upstream server refs for virtual mode. Each Ref is an Id in IMcpServerRegistry.

name.description: The tool name as exposed to the agent.

from.description: The Id of the source server that provides this tool.

sourceToolName.description: Optional override for the upstream tool name if it differs from Name.

toolProjection.description: Tool projection for virtual mode. Maps visible tool name → source server id. Null = expose all tools from all sources (de-duplicated by name, first source wins).

mcpGatewayRef.description: Optional Id applied per tool dispatch through this server. Lower precedence than an agent-level McpGatewayRef.

ontologyRef.description: Optional name of a deployment-supplied domain-ontology artifact to bind to this server. When set, the south cartridge resolves the artifact through IDomainOntologyArtifactRegistry and shapes the server's tools/list + tool-call dispatch with its tags, descriptions, and cross-refs. Missing or unknown refs degrade gracefully (no cartridge applied — passthrough). Plan C1-7.

failureOntologyRef.description: Optional name of a deployment-supplied FailureAttributionArtifact (Part 2b). When set, AgentManifestTranslator appends a FailureAttributionEnricher to the per-agent tool-gateway chain. Failed tool calls through this server emit failure.attribution trajectory events with concept and path from the artifact. Missing or unknown refs degrade gracefully (no enricher applied).
